verdict.reasoning: InsecureAirdrop.receiveAirdrop() performs an external call inside the canReceiveAirdrop() modifier before it updates userBalances[msg.sender] and receivedAirdrops[msg.sender] at lines 27-28. Because neverReceiveAirdrop() only checks the flag before _; and the flag is set after the external call, a malicious receiver can reenter via its canReceiveAirdrop() callback and pass the modifier again. This is a reentrancy-via-modifier pattern, and it can also affect the shared receivedAirdrops state used by hasReceivedAirdrop() and neverReceiveAirdrop().
